raw_text_excerpt: From the Pricing page (platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/pricing) model-pricing table verbatim: 'Claude Opus 4.7 | $5 / MTok | $6.25 / MTok | $10 / MTok | $0.50 / MTok | $25 / MTok'; 'Claude Sonnet 4.6 | $3 / MTok | $3.75 / MTok | $6 / MTok | $0.30 / MTok | $15 / MTok'; 'Claude Haiku 4.5 | $1 / MTok | $1.25 / MTok | $2 / MTok | $0.10 / MTok | $5 / MTok'. Columns: Base Input Tokens | 5m Cache Writes | 1h Cache Writes | Cache Hits & Refreshes | Output Tokens. Corroborated on the Models Overview page (platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview) latest-models-comparison Pricing row: 'Claude Opus 4.7: $5 / input MTok, $25 / output MTok'; 'Claude Sonnet 4.6: $3 / input MTok, $15 / output MTok'; 'Claude Haiku 4.5: $1 / input MTok, $5 / output MTok'. Legacy-models table shows Claude Opus 4.1 + Claude Opus 4 (deprecated) at $15 / input MTok and $75 / output MTok, consistent with the M10 attestation epoch. New observation at 2026-05-22 (not present at the M11a-extension 2026-05-15 attestation): the pricing table now lists Claude Opus 4.5 and Claude Opus 4.6 alongside Claude Opus 4.7 at $5 / $25 (same rate); Claude Sonnet 4.5 + Sonnet 4.6 at $3 / $15. These new rows do not affect M11a-extension-extension internal cost modeling since the milestone uses only claude-opus-4-7, claude-sonnet-4-6, and claude-haiku-4-5. Additional new note on the Pricing page: 'Opus 4.7 uses a new tokenizer compared to previous models, contributing to its improved performance on a wide range of tasks. This new tokenizer may use up to 35% more tokens for the same fixed text.' — informational; the per-MTok rate is unchanged so absolute Opus token-cost numbers could differ from pre-Opus-4.7 epochs by the tokenizer factor but the Pareto and per-trace cost ratios reported in this milestone are insensitive to a uniform input-token-count factor.
notes: Pricing attestation archived at M11a-extension-extension Commit A per pre-reg ~/.claude/plans/m11a-extension-extension-n40.md §D11 HARD GATE + §D14 hardening pass. Three findings: (1) Claude Opus 4.7 observed-published rate $5/$25 — BYTEWISE IDENTICAL to M11a-extension attestation runs/data/21a-pricing-attestation-2026-05-15.json (no rate rotation in the 7-day interval). Per pre-reg §D11: M10 lock ($15 input / $75 output) HOLDS for all M11a-extension-extension internal cost modeling to preserve cross-milestone consistency with M10/M10b/M11a/M11b/M11a-extension cost numbers in runs/17,18,19,20,21. agent/arbiter.py OPUS_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 15.0 + OPUS_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 75.0 remain unchanged through Commit B (V4 wiring frozen bytewise from M11a-extension SHA 09be309d... per §D1+§D14-H4 dual gate). (2) Claude Sonnet 4.6 rate $3 / $15 — BYTEWISE IDENTICAL to M11a-extension + M11b attestations; matches agent/arbiter.py SONNET_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 3.0 + SONNET_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 15.0; no plan §D10 update needed. (3) Claude Haiku 4.5 rate $1 / $5 — BYTEWISE IDENTICAL to M11a-extension + M11b attestations; matches agent/arbiter.py HAIKU_INPUT_USD_PER_M = 1.0 + HAIKU_OUTPUT_USD_PER_M = 5.0; no plan §D10 update needed. Zero observed-published-rate drift across the FULL milestone chain M11b Commit A (2026-05-13) → M11a-extension Commit A (2026-05-15) → M11a-extension-extension Commit A (2026-05-22) — 9-day cumulative interval covers three consecutive empirical milestone Commit A landings. Cross-tier Pareto ratios reported in this milestone (V2-Opus vs V4-Opus matched-arbiter; V4-Opus vs V4-Sonnet vs V4-Haiku cross-tier; combined-N=40 sample) are insensitive to a uniform Opus-rate scaling factor — re-pricing Opus to $5/$25 would scale all Opus-tier absolute $/hit numbers by 1/3 but leave matched-arbiter and cross-tier ratios unchanged at the per-trace level. Absolute Opus-tier $/hit numbers in M11a-extension-extension's paper appendix are reported at the M10-locked rate; the paper appendix reproduces the M11b/M11a-extension caveat verbatim that deployment-cost-projection should divide Opus-tier absolute numbers by ~3 for current-Anthropic-rate-deployment alignment. M11a-extension-extension is the CI-tightening sample-size extension milestone (NOT the final empirical milestone — the surprise-gate-bypass residual + V5 prompt revision named in Future Work remain post-M11a-extension-extension work); preserving M10 lock through paper-line cost numbers maintains internal consistency through the complete M8b → M10 → M10b → M11a → M11b → M11a-extension → M11a-extension-extension milestone series. The §D9 Surface #13 Layer-1 cross-milestone Claude alias drift defense rests on (a) this attestation showing zero published-rate drift, and (b) Commit B's belt-and-suspenders bit-identical V4-Opus + V4-Sonnet + V4-Haiku + V2-Opus re-run against M11a-extension's runs/data/21d-content-{opus,sonnet,haiku}-v{2,4}-*.json (V4-Opus + V2-Opus = HALT-gate on FAIL per §D13 Commit B verdict policy; V4-Sonnet + V4-Haiku = OBSERVATIONAL per D14-H5).
